$comment: Typography tokens. Path = semantic name (e.g. font.size.body). Size scale matches Tailwind's defaults so utilities like text-base / text-xl map cleanly. Code source of truth: packages/ui/src/tokens/tokens.json (family + scale).

sans.usage: Default for everything — body, UI, headings.
sans.notes: Inter Variable is loaded by apps/web at /apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx via next/font.

mono.usage: Code blocks, API examples, ID-style identifiers.
mono.notes: Not used in product surfaces; reserved for technical content.

caption.usage: Captions, fine-print, table footers. Below body in hierarchy.
caption.notes: Tailwind: text-xs. Never use for body text on mobile.

small.usage: Hints, helper text in forms, metadata in dense lists.
small.notes: Tailwind: text-sm. Below 16px on mobile triggers iOS input zoom — never use for <input> text.

body.usage: Default body text. Minimum size for prose on mobile.
body.notes: Tailwind: text-base. Inputs use this size to avoid iOS zoom.

heading.notes: Tailwind: text-2xl. Default page title weight.

headingLarge.notes: Tailwind: text-3xl. Use tabular-nums when displaying numbers.

display.usage: Hero KPI numbers (in-session set counter), feature spotlights.
display.notes: Tailwind: text-4xl. Mobile: in-session set counter steps up to text-6xl (60px).

displayLarge.notes: Tailwind: text-5xl. Step up to displayXLarge on md+.

displayXLarge.usage: Marketing hero H1 on desktop, in-session set counter on mobile.

medium.usage: Form labels, table headers, mild emphasis inside body text.

semibold.notes: Tailwind: font-semibold. Default for headings and primary CTAs.

bold.notes: Avoid in product surfaces — semibold + size is usually enough.

snug.usage: Sub-headings, button labels, single-line UI text.

relaxed.usage: Long-form reading surfaces: marketing body, help articles.

normal.usage: Default for body and most headings.

wide.usage: Uppercase labels (KPI category, table headers, tab labels).
wide.notes: Tailwind: tracking-wide. Pair with text-xs uppercase.

tabularNums.usage: Any column of numbers: set counts, weights, durations, money.
tabularNums.notes: Tailwind: tabular-nums. Align decimals by tying with text-right where applicable.
